channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hillary Clinton, Condoleezza Rice commend Trump over Israel-Hamas deal
item.description: Former Secretaries of State Hillary Clinton and Condoleezza Rice on Friday commended President Trump for successfully facilitating the first stages of a ceasefire deal with Israel and Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: France's re-appointed prime minister Lecornu calls for calm amid political chaos
item.description: France’s newly re-appointed Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu acknowledged that he might be able to stay in his job long given the country's deep political divides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins and US troops land in Israel as ceasefire holds
item.description: Tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians are returning to their neighborhoods in Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is now in its second day.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Kareem Hana, Sam Metz, Sarah El Deeb And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power has been restored to over 800,000 residents in Kyiv, a day after Russia launched major attacks on Ukraine's power grid, causing widespread blackouts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has showcased his military's most powerful weapons at a massive parade attended by foreign leaders.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung And Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza were starting to walk north to return to their homes Friday, after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that its soldiers were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to visit Egypt and Israel early next week amid ceasefire deal in Middle East
item.description: President Trump said Friday he plans to visit Egypt and Israel during a one-day trip to the Middle East early next week to mark the implementation of a peace agreement that will see a pause in fighting in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts as 'really doing a lot' to resolve global crises and conflicts
item.description: President Donald Trump's efforts to negotiate peace deals, including the Israel-Hamas truce, received praise from Russian President Vladimir Putin at a summit in Tajikistan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces 100 percent tariff on China in response to rare earth controls
item.description: President Trump announced Friday he will raise tariffs on China in response to a move from Beijing to tighten its control over certain critical minerals and rare earth elements. Trump announced on Truth Social that he would impose a tariff of 100 percent on Chinese goods beginning Nov. 1 or sooner.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize bets on Polymarket raise leak concerns, prompts probe
item.description: The last-minute bets, first reported by local outlets, are a cause for concern for the Norwegian officials in charge of awarding the honor
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Dutch far-right politician Wilders fears he may have been a target of Belgian attack plotters
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Dutch far-right politician Geert Wilders said Friday that he’s suspending campaigning for national elections later this month following reports that he was a possible target of a suspected plot in Belgium to kill politicians using a drone carrying explosives.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, The Hill

item.title: Russian strikes wound at least 20 in Ukraine's capital as child is killed in separate attack
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drone and missile strikes wounded at least 20 people in Kyiv, damaged residential buildings and caused blackouts across swaths of Ukraine early Friday, authorities said. A child also was killed in separate attacks in the southeast of the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Republican leaders backed Venezuela's Machado for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Sen. Rick Scott, R-Fla., signed a letter supporting opposition leader María Corina Machado.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Nobel winner dedicates peace prize to people of Venezuela, Trump
item.description: This year's Nobel Peace Prize winner, Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado, has dedicated her award to the people of her country and to President Trump for his "decisive support” for democracy in Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Surina Venkat, The Hill

item.title: The Taliban government blames Pakistan for a strike on Kabul
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The Taliban government on Friday blamed Pakistan for striking the Afghan capital and bombing a market in the country's east, accusing its neighbor of violating national airspace. A blast struck before 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Burkina Faso rejects proposal to accept deportees from the US
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Burkina Faso says it has refused a proposal from the Trump administration to accept deportees from the United States. The West African country was asked whether it would accept non-citizens expelled by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Citing unease over graft, Japan's Komeito leaves the longstanding ruling coalition headed by the LDP
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The head of Japan's Komeito says it is leaving the ruling coalition headed by the Liberal Democratic Party due to concerns over corruption, in a major setback for the woman who hopes to become the country's next prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tony Blair is revered in Kosovo for helping end its war. Many ask if he can succeed again in Gaza
item.description: PRISTINA, Kosovo (AP) — A U.S. peace plan has propelled former British Prime Minister Tony Blair to the forefront of efforts to end the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza. While his legacy in the Middle East is controversial, especially given his role in taking the U.K. to war as part of the 2003 U.S.
item.dc:creator: FLORENT BAJRAMI and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows to build a 'socialist paradise' ahead of a likely military parade
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un lauded what he called his country's fight against a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: Philippine seismologists say that a new offshore earthquake with a preliminary 6.8 magnitude hit the same southern region struck earlier by an earthquake with a magnitude of 7.4.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: Israel and Hamas agree to a ceasefire, release of hostages and prisoners
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to a pause in their devastating two-year war and the release of the remaining hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Peace celebrations in Gaza marred by daunting rebuilding phase to come
item.description: A freelance photographer based in Gaza details his hope for life in the war-torn city now that a ceasefire deal has been reached.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Farm workers forced into slavery, Greek police say
item.description: ATHENS, Oct 9 (REUTERS) - Greece has dismantled a criminal ring which trafficked dozens of Nepali men, beat them and forced them to work in farms across the country in degrading conditions with little or no payment, police said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Yannis Souliotis and Renee Maltezou, NewsNation

item.title: Manchester synagogue attacker 'pledged allegiance to Islamic State,' police say
item.description: The suspect in the deadly attack on a synagogue in Manchester allegedly pledged allegiance to the Islamic State in a call to emergency dispatchers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greta Thunberg slammed for using image of starved Israeli hostage to show Palestinians suffering in Gaza
item.description: Greta Thunberg is being criticized after using a photo of starving hostage Evyatar David in an Instagram post claiming to show Palestinians suffering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel seizes another Gaza flotilla
item.description: Israel intercepted another flotilla attempting to break the naval blockade and reach Gaza. This comes just days after another fleet was intercepted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump thanks group representing families of hostages for Nobel Peace Prize appeal
item.description: President Donald Trump thanked the Hostages and Missing Families Forum for its appeal to the Nobel committee to award the president the Nobel Peace Prize.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big Time Rush's James Maslow releases new song celebrating Israel
item.description: Maslow acknowledged he chose Oct. 7th because of how the "country (Israel) and this world have normalized a hate towards each other.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Jilly Cooper, British author of bestselling risqué novels, including 'Rivals,' dies at 88
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Jilly Cooper, the bestselling British author known for her chronicles of class and sex in risqué novels, including “Rivals” and “Riders,” has died unexpectedly, her agent and family said Monday. She was 88.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rescuers in India search for missing after landslides kill 24 in Darjeeling region
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Rescuers were scouring India's northeastern tea-growing hill district of Darjeeling on Monday for those reported missing a day after landslides triggered by heavy downpours killed at least 24 people, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French Prime Minister resigns after less than a month in office
item.description: Facing criticism from all sides, France’s new prime minister Sébastien Lecornu resigned less than 24 hours after naming his government
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel and Hamas prepare for negotiations in Egypt ahead of possible Gaza ceasefire
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel and Hamas prepared for indirect negotiations in Egypt on Monday, as hopes for a possible ceasefire in Gaza grew after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said a hostage release could be announced this week. Tuesday marks two years since the Hamas attack that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
